questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: For $n \in \mathrm{N}$, if $\cot ^{-1} 3+\cot ^{-1} 4+\cot ^{-1} 5+\cot ^{-1} n=\frac{\pi}{4}$, then $n$ is equal to_____

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given that the inverse trigonometric function assumes principal values only. Let $x$, $y$ be any two real numbers in $[-1,1]$ such that $\cos ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} y=\alpha, \frac{-\pi}{2} \leq \alpha \leq \pi$.
Then, the minimum value of $x^2+y^2+2 x y \sin \alpha$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \cos ^{-1} x-\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\cos ^{-1} y\right)=\alpha \\ & \cos ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} y=\frac{\pi}{2}+\alpha \\ & \alpha \in\left[-\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi\right], \frac{\pi}{2}+\alpha \in\left[0, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right] \\ & \cos ^{-1}\left(x y-\sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-y^2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}+\alpha \\ & x y-\sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-y^2}=-\sin \alpha \\ & (x y+\sin \alpha)=\left(1-x^2\right)\left(1-y^2\right) \\ & x^2 y^2+2 x y \sin a+\sin ^2 a=1-x^2-y^2+x^2 y^2 \\ & x^2+y^2+2 x y \sin \alpha=1-\sin ^2 \alpha \\ & x^2+y^2+2 x y \sin \alpha=\cos ^2 \alpha \end{aligned}$
Min. value of $\cos ^2 \alpha=0$ At $\alpha=\frac{\pi}{2}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Let $|\cos \theta \cos (60-\theta) \cos (60+\theta)| \leq \frac{1}{8}, \theta \epsilon[0,2 \pi]$. Then, the sum of all $\theta \epsilon[0,2 \pi]$, where $\cos 3 \theta$ attains its maximum value, is :
questions.solution:
We know that
$(\cos \theta)\left(\cos( 6 0 ^ { \circ } - \theta ) \left(\cos \left(60^{\circ}+\theta\right)=\frac{1}{4} \cos 3 \theta\right.\right.$
So equation reduces to $\left|\frac{1}{4} \cos 3 \theta\right| \leq \frac{1}{8}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow|\cos 3 \theta| \leq \frac{1}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow-\frac{1}{2} \leq \cos 3 \theta \leq \frac{1}{2}
\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow$ maximum value of $\cos 3 \theta=\frac{1}{2}$, here
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow 3 \theta=2 \mathrm{n} \pi \pm \frac{\pi}{3} \\
& \theta=\frac{2 \mathrm{n} \pi}{3} \pm \frac{\pi}{9}
\end{aligned}$
As $\theta \in[0,2 \pi]$ possible values are
$\theta=\left\{\frac{\pi}{9}, \frac{5 \pi}{9}, \frac{7 \pi}{9}, \frac{11 \pi}{9}, \frac{13 \pi}{9}, \frac{17 \pi}{9}\right\}$
Whose sum is
$\frac{\pi}{9}+\frac{5 \pi}{9}+\frac{7 \pi}{9}+\frac{11 \pi}{9}+\frac{13 \pi}{9}+\frac{17 \pi}{9}=\frac{54 \pi}{9}=6 \pi$

questions.q: If the value of $\frac{3 \cos 36^{\circ}+5 \sin 18^{\circ}}{5 \cos 36^{\circ}-3 \sin 18^{\circ}}$ is $\frac{a \sqrt{5}-b}{c}$, where $a, b, c$ are natural numbers and $\operatorname{gcd}(a, c)=1$, then $a+b+c$ is equal to :
